name: facebook_posts_scraper
title: Facebook Posts Scraper
description: Extract public Facebook Page Plugin timeline posts from a page URL or slug, including visible post text, post URLs, timestamps, reactions, comments, shares, media, and outbound links.

seo.title: Facebook Posts Scraper Tool for Public Page Timelines
seo.description: Scrape public Facebook Page Plugin timeline posts with Better Fetch, including post text, URLs, timestamps, reaction counts, comment counts, share counts, media, and links.
seo.intro: Facebook Posts Scraper gives agents a validated no-login slice of public Facebook Page monitoring. Provide a Facebook Page URL or slug and the tool renders Facebook's public Page Plugin timeline, then normalizes the visible post cards into compact records with author, post URL, timestamp label, text, engagement counts, media thumbnails, and outbound links. It is designed for social listening, brand monitoring, campaign research, newsroom checks, and lightweight competitor tracking where a bounded public page feed is enough.

useCases.title: Brand activity monitoring
useCases.description: Watch public Page posts for recent announcements, campaigns, product launches, and engagement signals without maintaining a Facebook login session.

useCases.title: Social listening research
useCases.description: Collect visible post text, post URLs, reactions, comments, shares, media, and outbound links for an agent that compares public Facebook activity across brands.

useCases.title: Newsroom and campaign checks
useCases.description: Pull the latest visible public posts from official Pages so editorial, comms, and marketing workflows can cite the source post and summarize engagement quickly.

faqs.question: Does Facebook Posts Scraper need a Facebook login?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 uses Facebook's public Page Plugin timeline through Better Fetch. It only returns posts visible through that public embed surface.

faqs.question: Can it scrape private profiles, groups, comments, or deep pagination?
faqs.answer: Not in this first slice. Version 0.1 is limited to public Page Plugin-visible timeline cards and does not use cookies, account data, private groups, or authenticated endpoints.

faqs.question: Why do some public Pages return fewer posts?
faqs.answer: Facebook controls what the Page Plugin exposes. Age-restricted, country-restricted, unsupported, or temporarily unavailable Pages may show only headers or fewer post cards.

page_url.description: Public Facebook Page URL, e.g. https://www.facebook.com/NASA. Use page_url or page_slug.

page_slug.description: Facebook Page slug, numeric page ID, or page path, e.g. NASA. Use page_slug or page_url.

max_results.description: Maximum visible timeline posts to return from the public Page Plugin.

wait_ms.description: Extra milliseconds to wait after the plugin page loads.

page_url.description: Canonical public Facebook Page URL requested

count.description: Number of returned visible post records

posts.description: Public Facebook Page timeline post records visible in the plugin

post_url.description: Canonical Facebook post, reel, or photo URL

posted_at_label.description: Visible relative or absolute timestamp label

posted_at.description: ISO timestamp when Facebook exposes a unix timestamp

text.description: Visible post text with HTML tags removed

external_links.description: Comma-separated outbound links visible in the post text
